content:
# Exodus Chapter 24 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~46r–46v (PDF pp. 90–91); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 66–67 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_024_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_024_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_024_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_024_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE COVENANT RATIFICATION:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 24 in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter forms the climactic covenant ratification that seals the Book of the Covenant (20:22–23:33). It moves through three extraordinary scenes: the blood ceremony binding YHWH and Israel (vv. 1–8), the theophanic meal where the elders see God and live (vv. 9–11), and Moses' ascent into the cloud-covered mountain for forty days (vv. 12–18). The chapter is marked by a striking narrative disjunction between vv. 1–2 (the command to ascend) and vv. 3–8 (the altar ceremony at the base), with the actual ascent delayed until v. 9. The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 66–67) presents the LXX rendering in continuous uncial script; the LXX versification aligns with the MT throughout this chapter, though the LXX adds notable expansions at several points.

---

## The Summons to Ascend (vv. 1–2)

**1** And to Moses [ve-el Mosheh] he said [amar], "Come up [aleh] to [el] YHWH, you [attah] and Aaron [ve-Aharon], Nadab [Nadav] and Abihu [va-Avihu], and seventy [ve-shiv'im] of the elders [mi-ziqnei] of Israel [Yisra'el], and you shall bow down [ve-hishtakhavitem] from afar [me-rakhoq].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶל־מֹשֶׁה אָמַר עֲלֵה אֶל־יְהוָה אַתָּה וְאַהֲרֹן נָדָב וַאֲבִיהוּא וְשִׁבְעִים מִזִּקְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְהִשְׁתַּחֲוִיתֶם מֵרָחֹק. **Key terms:** עֲלֵה (aleh) = qal imperative of עלה (alah, "to go up, ascend") — the summons to the mountain. מִזִּקְנֵי (mi-ziqnei) = "from the elders of" — the partitive min; seventy selected from the larger body of elders. מֵרָחֹק (me-rakhoq) = "from afar, at a distance" — the elders must worship at a distance; only Moses may approach. The subject of "he said" is ambiguous — the chapter opens mid-speech, continuing from the covenant legislation. LXX: καὶ Μωυσῇ εἶπεν ἀνάβηθι πρὸς κύριον — ἀναβαίνω ("go up") for aleh.

**2** And Moses [ve-niggash Mosheh] alone [levaddo] shall draw near [ve-niggash] to [el] YHWH, and they [ve-hem] shall not draw near [lo yiggashu], and the people [ve-ha-am] shall not go up [lo ya'alu] with him [immo]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנִגַּשׁ מֹשֶׁה לְבַדּוֹ אֶל־יְהוָה וְהֵם לֹא יִגָּשׁוּ וְהָעָם לֹא יַעֲלוּ עִמּוֹ. **Key terms:** וְנִגַּשׁ (ve-niggash) = niphal perfect of נגשׁ (nagash, "to draw near, approach") — "and he shall draw near." לְבַדּוֹ (levaddo) = "alone, by himself" — from בדד (badad, "to be alone"); Moses' solitary access to YHWH distinguishes him from all others, including the priests and elders. Three concentric zones of holiness: the people at the base, the elders partway up, Moses alone at the summit. LXX: καὶ ἐγγιεῖ Μωυσῆς μόνος πρὸς τὸν θεόν — ἐγγίζω ("draw near") for ve-niggash.

---

## The Blood of the Covenant (vv. 3–8)

**3** And Moses came [va-yavo Mosheh] and he recounted [va-yesapper] to the people [la-am] all [et kol] the words [divrei] of YHWH and all [ve-et kol] the judgments [ha-mishpatim], and all [kol] the people [ha-am] answered [va-ya'an] with one [ekhad] voice [qol] and they said [va-yomeru], "All [kol] the words [ha-devarim] which [asher] YHWH has spoken [dibber], we will do [na'aseh]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיָּבֹא מֹשֶׁה וַיְסַפֵּר לָעָם אֵת כָּל־דִּבְרֵי יְהוָה וְאֵת כָּל־הַמִּשְׁפָּטִים וַיַּעַן כָּל־הָעָם קוֹל אֶחָד וַיֹּאמְרוּ כָּל־הַדְּבָרִים אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּר יְהוָה נַעֲשֶׂה. **Key terms:** וַיְסַפֵּר (va-yesapper) = piel imperfect of ספר (safar, "to recount, declare, tell") — Moses transmits the covenant terms orally. קוֹל אֶחָד (qol ekhad) = "one voice" — unanimity of consent; the people ratify the covenant as a body. נַעֲשֶׂה (na'aseh) = "we will do" — qal imperfect 1cp of עשׂה; the first of two declarations of obedience (cf. v. 7, na'aseh ve-nishma). LXX: καὶ ἀπεκρίθη πᾶς ὁ λαὸς φωνῇ μιᾷ — φωνὴ μία ("one voice") for qol ekhad.

**4** And Moses wrote [va-yikhtov Mosheh] all [et kol] the words [divrei] of YHWH, and he rose early [va-yashkem] in the morning [ba-boqer] and he built [va-yiven] an altar [mizbeakh] under [takhat] the mountain [ha-har] and twelve [shtem esreh] pillars [mattsevah] for the twelve [li-shnem asar] tribes [shivtei] of Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּכְתֹּב מֹשֶׁה אֵת כָּל־דִּבְרֵי יְהוָה וַיַּשְׁכֵּם בַּבֹּקֶר וַיִּבֶן מִזְבֵּחַ תַּחַת הָהָר וּשְׁתֵּים עֶשְׂרֵה מַצֵּבָה לִשְׁנֵים עָשָׂר שִׁבְטֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל. **Key terms:** וַיִּכְתֹּב (va-yikhtov) = qal imperfect of כתב (katav, "to write") — the first explicit mention of Moses writing YHWH's words; this written document is the "Book of the Covenant" (sefer ha-berit) of v. 7. מַצֵּבָה (mattsevah) = "standing stone, pillar" — here used positively as covenant witnesses representing each tribe, unlike the forbidden Canaanite pillars of 23:24. LXX: καὶ ἔγραψεν Μωυσῆς πάντα τὰ ῥήματα κυρίου — ῥῆμα ("word, utterance") for divrei.

**5** And he sent [va-yishlakh] the young men [et-na'arei] of the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el], and they offered up [va-ya'alu] burnt offerings [olot], and they slaughtered [va-yizbekhu] sacrifices [zevakhim] — peace offerings [shelamim] — to YHWH [la-YHWH]: bulls [parim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּשְׁלַח אֶת־נַעֲרֵי בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וַיַּעֲלוּ עֹלֹת וַיִּזְבְּחוּ זְבָחִים שְׁלָמִים לַיהוָה פָּרִים. **Key terms:** נַעֲרֵי (na'arei) = "young men of" — from נער (na'ar, "youth, young man"); these serve as sacrificial assistants before the Levitical priesthood is established. עֹלֹת (olot) = "burnt offerings" — from עלה (alah, "to go up"); the whole-burnt offering ascends entirely in smoke. שְׁלָמִים (shelamim) = "peace offerings, well-being offerings" — from שׁלם (shalam, "to be complete, whole"); the communion sacrifice whose meat is shared. פָּרִים (parim) = "bulls, young bulls" — the costliest sacrifice. LXX: καὶ ἀπέστειλεν τοὺς νεανίσκους τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ — νεανίσκος ("young man") for na'arei.

**6** And Moses took [va-yiqqakh Mosheh] half [khatsi] of the blood [ha-dam] and he put [va-yasem] [it] in basins [ba-agganot], and half [va-khatsi] of the blood [ha-dam] he threw [zaraq] upon [al] the altar [ha-mizbeakh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקַּח מֹשֶׁה חֲצִי הַדָּם וַיָּשֶׂם בָּאַגָּנֹת וַחֲצִי הַדָּם זָרַק עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ. **Key terms:** חֲצִי (khatsi) = "half" — from חצה (khatsah, "to divide in two"); the blood is divided into two equal portions for a two-part ceremony. אַגָּנֹת (agganot) = "basins, bowls" — from אגן (aggan, "a basin"); vessels for collecting sacrificial blood. זָרַק (zaraq) = qal perfect of זרק (zaraq, "to throw, toss, sprinkle") — not gentle sprinkling but vigorous throwing of blood against the altar. The altar represents YHWH's side of the covenant. LXX: καὶ λαβὼν Μωυσῆς τὸ ἥμισυ τοῦ αἵματος ἐνέχεεν εἰς κρατῆρας — κρατήρ ("mixing bowl") for agganot.

**7** And he took [va-yiqqakh] the Book [sefer] of the Covenant [ha-berit] and he read [va-yiqra] in the ears [be-oznei] of the people [ha-am], and they said [va-yomeru], "All [kol] that [asher] YHWH has spoken [dibber] we will do [na'aseh] and we will hear [ve-nishma]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקַּח סֵפֶר הַבְּרִית וַיִּקְרָא בְּאָזְנֵי הָעָם וַיֹּאמְרוּ כֹּל אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּר יְהוָה נַעֲשֶׂה וְנִשְׁמָע. **Key terms:** סֵפֶר הַבְּרִית (sefer ha-berit) = "the scroll/book of the covenant" — the written document from v. 4. נַעֲשֶׂה וְנִשְׁמָע (na'aseh ve-nishma) = "we will do and we will hear/obey" — the famous declaration placing doing before hearing. The verb שׁמע (shama) means not merely auditory hearing but obedient heeding. The sequence has generated extensive rabbinic discussion: Israel committed to obedience before fully understanding the implications. LXX: πάντα ὅσα ἐλάλησεν κύριος ποιήσομεν καὶ ἀκουσόμεθα — ἀκούω ("hear, obey") for nishma.

**8** And Moses took [va-yiqqakh Mosheh] the blood [ha-dam] and he threw [va-yizroq] [it] upon [al] the people [ha-am], and he said [va-yomer], "Behold [hinneh] the blood [dam] of the covenant [ha-berit] which [asher] YHWH has cut [karat] with you [immakhem] upon [al] all [kol] these [ha-devarim] words [ha-elleh]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקַּח מֹשֶׁה אֶת־הַדָּם וַיִּזְרֹק עַל־הָעָם וַיֹּאמֶר הִנֵּה דַם־הַבְּרִית אֲשֶׁר כָּרַת יְהוָה עִמָּכֶם עַל כָּל־הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה. **Key terms:** וַיִּזְרֹק (va-yizroq) = qal imperfect of זרק (zaraq, "to throw, toss") — the second half of the blood is thrown upon the people. The two-part blood ceremony binds both parties: half on the altar (YHWH's side), half on the people (Israel's side). דַם־הַבְּרִית (dam ha-berit) = "blood of the covenant" — this phrase is cited by Jesus at the Last Supper (Matt 26:28; Mark 14:24) and in Hebrews 9:20. כָּרַת (karat) = qal perfect of כרת (karat, "to cut") — covenants are "cut." LXX: ἰδοὺ τὸ αἷμα τῆς διαθήκης ἧς διέθετο κύριος πρὸς ὑμᾶς — διαθήκη ("covenant") for berit.

---

## The Elders See God (vv. 9–11)

**9** And Moses went up [va-ya'al Mosheh], and Aaron [ve-Aharon], Nadab [Nadav] and Abihu [va-Avihu], and seventy [ve-shiv'im] of the elders [mi-ziqnei] of Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַל מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן נָדָב וַאֲבִיהוּא וְשִׁבְעִים מִזִּקְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל. **Key terms:** וַיַּעַל (va-ya'al) = qal imperfect of עלה (alah, "to go up, ascend") — the actual ascent, fulfilling the command of v. 1. The party of seventy-four (Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders) now ascends. LXX: καὶ ἀνέβη Μωυσῆς καὶ Ααρων καὶ Ναδαβ καὶ Αβιουδ — the LXX lists the names identically.

**10** And they saw [va-yir'u] the God [et Elohei] of Israel [Yisra'el], and under [ve-takhat] his feet [raglav] [was] like a work [ke-ma'aseh] of the sapphire [ha-sappir] stone [livnat], and like the very [ke-etsem] heavens [ha-shamayim] for clearness [la-tohar].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּרְאוּ אֵת אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְתַחַת רַגְלָיו כְּמַעֲשֵׂה לִבְנַת הַסַּפִּיר וּכְעֶצֶם הַשָּׁמַיִם לָטֹהַר. **Key terms:** וַיִּרְאוּ אֵת אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (va-yir'u et Elohei Yisra'el) = "and they saw the God of Israel" — an astonishing statement; the verb ראה (ra'ah, "to see") is used directly with God as object. לִבְנַת הַסַּפִּיר (livnat ha-sappir) = "the sapphire pavement/brickwork" — לִבְנָה (livnah) may derive from לבן (lavan, "white, brick") — either "whiteness/brilliance of sapphire" or "pavement/brickwork of sapphire." כְּעֶצֶם הַשָּׁמַיִם (ke-etsem ha-shamayim) = "like the very bone/substance of the heavens" — etsem means "bone, substance, self, essence"; the sky at its most pure and essential. לָטֹהַר (la-tohar) = "for clearness, for purity" — from טהר (tahar, "to be clean, pure"). LXX: καὶ εἶδον τὸν τόπον οὗ εἱστήκει ἐκεῖ ὁ θεὸς τοῦ Ισραηλ — the LXX reads "the place where the God of Israel stood" rather than directly "the God of Israel," softening the anthropomorphism. ὡσεὶ ἔργον πλίνθου σαπφείρου — πλίνθος ("brick") for livnat.

**11** And against [ve-el] the nobles [atsilei] of the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el] he did not send [lo shalakh] his hand [yado], and they beheld [va-yekhezu] God [et-ha-Elohim] and they ate [va-yokhelu] and they drank [va-yishtu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶל־אֲצִילֵי בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לֹא שָׁלַח יָדוֹ וַיֶּחֱזוּ אֶת־הָאֱלֹהִים וַיֹּאכְלוּ וַיִּשְׁתּוּ. **Key terms:** אֲצִילֵי (atsilei) = "nobles, chosen ones" — from אצל (atsal, "to set aside, reserve"); those set apart for honor. A different word from ziqnei ("elders") in vv. 1, 9. לֹא שָׁלַח יָדוֹ (lo shalakh yado) = "he did not send his hand" — i.e., God did not strike them; the implication is that seeing God should have been lethal (cf. 33:20). וַיֶּחֱזוּ (va-yekhezu) = qal imperfect of חזה (khazah, "to behold, envision, gaze") — a different verb than ראה (ra'ah) in v. 10; khazah often carries prophetic or visionary overtones. The juxtaposition of divine vision and communal eating is remarkable — a covenant meal in the divine presence. LXX: καὶ τῶν ἐπιλέκτων τοῦ Ισραηλ οὐ διεφώνησεν οὐδὲ εἷς — ἐπίλεκτος ("chosen, select") for atsilei; the LXX reads "not one disagreed/perished" rather than "he did not send his hand."

---

## Moses Ascends for the Stone Tablets (vv. 12–14)

**12** And YHWH said [va-yomer YHWH] to [el] Moses [Mosheh], "Come up [aleh] to me [elai] to the mountain [ha-harah] and be [ve-heyeh] there [sham], and I will give [ve-ettenah] to you [lekha] the tablets [et-lukhot] of stone [ha-even] and the Torah [ve-ha-Torah] and the commandment [ve-ha-mitsvah] which [asher] I have written [katavti] to instruct them [le-horotam]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה עֲלֵה אֵלַי הָהָרָה וֶהְיֵה־שָׁם וְאֶתְּנָה לְךָ אֶת־לֻחֹת הָאֶבֶן וְהַתּוֹרָה וְהַמִּצְוָה אֲשֶׁר כָּתַבְתִּי לְהוֹרֹתָם. **Key terms:** לֻחֹת הָאֶבֶן (lukhot ha-even) = "the tablets of stone" — the first mention of the stone tablets. וְהַתּוֹרָה וְהַמִּצְוָה (ve-ha-Torah ve-ha-mitsvah) = "and the Torah and the commandment" — Torah from ירה (yarah, hiphil: "to teach, instruct, direct"); mitsvah from צוה (tsavah, "to command"). לְהוֹרֹתָם (le-horotam) = hiphil infinitive of ירה + 3mp suffix — "to instruct them"; the tablets are pedagogical. The relationship between "the Torah and the commandment" and the stone tablets is debated — are they identical, or does the phrase encompass additional instruction? LXX: καὶ δώσω σοι τὰ πυξία τὰ λίθινα, τὸν νόμον καὶ τὰς ἐντολάς — πυξίον ("writing tablet") for lukhot; νόμος ("law") for Torah.

**13** And Moses arose [va-yaqom Mosheh] and Joshua [vi-Yehoshua] his minister [mesharto], and Moses went up [va-ya'al Mosheh] to [el] the mountain [har] of God [ha-Elohim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיָּקָם מֹשֶׁה וִיהוֹשֻׁעַ מְשָׁרְתוֹ וַיַּעַל מֹשֶׁה אֶל־הַר הָאֱלֹהִים. **Key terms:** מְשָׁרְתוֹ (mesharto) = "his minister, his attendant" — piel participle of שׁרת (sharat, "to minister, serve"); Joshua serves Moses as a personal aide. הַר הָאֱלֹהִים (har ha-Elohim) = "the mountain of God" — i.e., Sinai/Horeb. Joshua's presence is mentioned without explanation of how far he ascends; he reappears in 32:17 waiting on the mountain when Moses descends. LXX: καὶ ἀναστὰς Μωυσῆς καὶ Ἰησοῦς ὁ παρεστηκὼς αὐτῷ — παρεστηκώς ("one standing beside") for mesharto.

**14** And to the elders [ve-el-ha-zeqenim] he said [amar], "Wait [shevu] for us [lanu] here [ba-zeh] until [ad] that [asher] we return [nashuv] to you [aleikhem], and behold [ve-hinneh], Aaron [Aharon] and Hur [ve-Khur] are with you [immakhem]; whoever [mi] has [ba'al] a matter [devarim] shall draw near [yiggash] to them [alehem]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶל־הַזְּקֵנִים אָמַר שְׁבוּ־לָנוּ בָזֶה עַד אֲשֶׁר־נָשׁוּב אֲלֵיכֶם וְהִנֵּה אַהֲרֹן וְחוּר עִמָּכֶם מִי־בַעַל דְּבָרִים יִגַּשׁ אֲלֵהֶם. **Key terms:** שְׁבוּ (shevu) = qal imperative of ישׁב (yashav, "to sit, dwell, remain") — "sit/wait." חוּר (Khur) = "Hur" — previously mentioned in 17:10, 12 holding up Moses' arms during the Amalekite battle; here appointed alongside Aaron as interim leader. בַּעַל דְּבָרִים (ba'al devarim) = "a possessor of words/matters" — i.e., one who has a legal case or dispute. LXX: καὶ τοῖς πρεσβυτέροις εἶπαν ἡσυχάζετε αὐτοῦ — ἡσυχάζω ("be still, remain quiet") for shevu.

---

## The Glory of YHWH on Mount Sinai (vv. 15–18)

**15** And Moses went up [va-ya'al Mosheh] to [el] the mountain [ha-har], and the cloud [he-anan] covered [va-yekhas] the mountain [et-ha-har].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַל מֹשֶׁה אֶל־הָהָר וַיְכַס הֶעָנָן אֶת־הָהָר. **Key terms:** וַיְכַס (va-yekhas) = piel imperfect of כסה (kasah, "to cover, conceal") — "and it covered." הֶעָנָן (he-anan) = "the cloud" — the theophanic cloud that both reveals and conceals YHWH's presence; the definite article indicates the specific cloud of divine presence already known from the Exodus narrative (cf. 13:21–22; 19:9, 16). LXX: καὶ ἀνέβη Μωυσῆς εἰς τὸ ὄρος καὶ ἐκάλυψεν ἡ νεφέλη τὸ ὄρος — νεφέλη ("cloud") for anan.

**16** And the glory [kevod] of YHWH settled [va-yishkon] upon [al] Mount [har] Sinai [Sinai], and the cloud [he-anan] covered it [va-yekhassehu] six [sheshet] days [yamim]; and he called [va-yiqra] to [el] Moses [Mosheh] on the seventh [ba-yom ha-shevi'i] day [yom] from the midst [mi-tokh] of the cloud [he-anan].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּשְׁכֹּן כְּבוֹד־יְהוָה עַל־הַר סִינַי וַיְכַסֵּהוּ הֶעָנָן שֵׁשֶׁת יָמִים וַיִּקְרָא אֶל־מֹשֶׁה בַּיּוֹם הַשְּׁבִיעִי מִתּוֹךְ הֶעָנָן. **Key terms:** וַיִּשְׁכֹּן (va-yishkon) = qal imperfect of שׁכן (shakhan, "to dwell, settle, tabernacle") — the root of שְׁכִינָה (Shekhinah), the indwelling divine presence; YHWH's glory "tabernacles" on the mountain, anticipating the tabernacle (mishkan) to be commanded in the following chapters. כְּבוֹד (kevod) = "glory, weight, heaviness" — from כבד (kavad, "to be heavy"); the visible, weighty manifestation of YHWH's presence. שֵׁשֶׁת יָמִים (sheshet yamim) = "six days" — Moses waits six days before being summoned on the seventh, echoing the creation pattern and the Sabbath structure. LXX: καὶ κατέβη ἡ δόξα τοῦ θεοῦ ἐπὶ τὸ ὄρος τὸ Σινα — note the LXX reads κατέβη ("descended") where the MT has va-yishkon ("settled/dwelt"), a significant difference in spatial metaphor.

**17** And the appearance [u-mar'eh] of the glory [kevod] of YHWH [was] like a consuming [ke-esh okhelet] fire [esh] on the head [be-rosh] of the mountain [ha-har] before the eyes [le-einei] of the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּמַרְאֵה כְּבוֹד יְהוָה כְּאֵשׁ אֹכֶלֶת בְּרֹאשׁ הָהָר לְעֵינֵי בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל. **Key terms:** מַרְאֵה (mar'eh) = "appearance, sight, vision" — from ראה (ra'ah, "to see"); what was visible to the eye. כְּאֵשׁ אֹכֶלֶת (ke-esh okhelet) = "like a fire consuming/devouring" — esh = "fire"; okhelet = qal participle feminine of אכל (akhal, "to eat, devour"). The glory does not merely shine — it devours. לְעֵינֵי (le-einei) = "before the eyes of" — the entire nation witnesses this theophany from the base of the mountain. LXX: τὸ δὲ εἶδος τῆς δόξης κυρίου ὡσεὶ πῦρ φλέγον ἐπὶ τῆς κορυφῆς τοῦ ὄρους — πῦρ φλέγον ("blazing fire") for esh okhelet; κορυφή ("summit") for rosh.

**18** And Moses came [va-yavo Mosheh] into the midst [be-tokh] of the cloud [he-anan] and he went up [va-ya'al] to [el] the mountain [ha-har], and Moses was [va-yehi Mosheh] on the mountain [ba-har] forty [arba'im] days [yom] and forty [ve-arba'im] nights [lailah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיָּבֹא מֹשֶׁה בְּתוֹךְ הֶעָנָן וַיַּעַל אֶל־הָהָר וַיְהִי מֹשֶׁה בָּהָר אַרְבָּעִים יוֹם וְאַרְבָּעִים לָיְלָה. **Key terms:** בְּתוֹךְ הֶעָנָן (be-tokh he-anan) = "into the midst of the cloud" — Moses enters the cloud-enveloped presence of God; what was death-zone for all others becomes his habitation. אַרְבָּעִים יוֹם וְאַרְבָּעִים לָיְלָה (arba'im yom ve-arba'im lailah) = "forty days and forty nights" — a formulaic period of divine encounter (cf. Elijah in 1 Kgs 19:8; Jesus' temptation in Matt 4:2). The duration signals a complete period of transformation and reception. During these forty days, Moses receives the tabernacle instructions (chs. 25–31). LXX: καὶ εἰσῆλθεν Μωυσῆς εἰς τὸ μέσον τῆς νεφέλης καὶ ἀνέβη εἰς τὸ ὄρος καὶ ἦν ἐκεῖ ἐν τῷ ὄρει τεσσεράκοντα ἡμέρας καὶ τεσσεράκοντα νύκτας — the LXX adds ἐκεῖ ("there"), a minor expansion.

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 24 is the hinge of the Pentateuch's covenant narrative, the ceremony that transforms spoken law into binding relationship sealed in blood. The chapter divides into three scenes that move progressively upward: the blood ritual at the base of the mountain (vv. 3–8), the theophanic meal partway up (vv. 9–11), and Moses' solitary entry into the cloud at the summit (vv. 15–18). Each scene intensifies the divine encounter — from hearing YHWH's words, to seeing YHWH's pavement, to dwelling within YHWH's glory.

The blood ceremony (vv. 6–8) is the only covenant ratification in the Hebrew Bible that explicitly divides sacrificial blood between altar and people. The phrase dam ha-berit ("blood of the covenant," v. 8) occurs only here in the Torah, making this the foundational text for understanding covenant as a bond of shared blood — a concept that carries directly into the New Testament (Heb 9:18–20). Moses' role is priestly though he holds no priestly office; the "young men" (na'arei, v. 5) perform the sacrifices before the Aaronic priesthood exists, and the entire scene stands outside the later cultic legislation.

The theophanic vision of vv. 9–11 is among the most extraordinary passages in Scripture. The text states flatly that seventy-four human beings "saw the God of Israel" (v. 10) — then immediately deflects the gaze downward to what is under his feet: the sapphire pavement (livnat ha-sappir) clear as the substance of the sky itself. The word livnat is a hapax legomenon; its meaning is debated between "brickwork" (connecting to Israel's brick-making slavery in Egypt, now transformed into heavenly splendor) and "brilliance/whiteness." The LXX notably softens v. 10, reading "they saw the place where the God of Israel stood" (τὸν τόπον οὗ εἱστήκει) rather than simply "they saw God" — a theological deflection to protect divine transcendence. The verb khazah (v. 11) shifts from ra'ah (v. 10), moving from direct seeing to prophetic beholding, while the statement "he did not send his hand" confirms the expectation that such seeing should have been fatal.

Key MT-LXX divergences: (1) In v. 10, the LXX inserts τὸν τόπον ("the place"), avoiding the direct statement that the elders saw God — the most significant theological divergence in the chapter. (2) In v. 11, the LXX reads οὐ διεφώνησεν οὐδὲ εἷς ("not one disagreed/perished") for lo shalakh yado ("he did not send his hand"), replacing the anthropomorphic idiom. (3) In v. 16, the LXX uses κατέβη ("descended") where the MT has va-yishkon ("settled/tabernacled"), substituting a spatial verb for a relational-theological one. (4) In v. 6, the LXX renders agganot ("basins") as κρατῆρες ("mixing bowls"), a Greek cultural equivalent. (5) In v. 12, the LXX translates lukhot as πυξία ("writing tablets") rather than the more common πλάκες ("flat stones/tablets"), a lexical peculiarity of the Vaticanus tradition at this point.

The six-day waiting period before the seventh-day summons (v. 16) recapitulates creation's Sabbath pattern: Moses waits in readiness as the glory settles, then is called on the seventh day into the consuming fire. The forty days and forty nights (v. 18) that follow will produce the tabernacle instructions — the architectural blueprint for making YHWH's mountain-top presence portable and permanent among Israel. The chapter thus bridges Sinai's theophany to the tabernacle's theology: what was experienced once on the mountain will be structured for ongoing habitation in the tent.
